ground_truth:
OPERATIVE NOTE

Date: 23/01/2024
Surgeon: Dr. Chris Taylor, MD
Procedure: Open Reduction Internal Fixation (ORIF) right distal
radius fracture
Anesthesia: Axillary block + sedation

Patient: Patricia Jones  DOB: 12/10/1960
MRN: 889456

Preop Dx: Comminuted distal radius fracture (Frykman type VIII)
Right dominant hand

Procedure:
Patient brought to OR. Axillary block placed by anesthesia.
Right upper extremity prepped and draped. Tourniquet inflated
to 250 mmHg.

Standard volar Henry approach utilized. Dissection through
tinterval between FCR and brachioradialis. Retraction of radial
artery. Fracture fragments identified. Extensive comminution
at the dorsal metaphyseal region.

Fracture reduced under fluoroscopic guidance. Volar locking
plate (Synthes DVR, 2.4mm) applied. Provisional fixation with
K-wires, then definitive screw fixation. Articular surface
assessed and found anatomic. Dorsal comminuted fragments
secured with additional screws through plate.

Fluoroscopy confirmed anatomic reduction. Wound irrigated.
Volar fascia closed with 3-0 Vicryl. Subcutaneous with 4-0
Vicryl. Skin with 4-0 Monocryl. Splint applied.

EBL: 50 mL
Complications: None

Postop Plan: Splint 2 weeks, then begin ROM.

Dr. Chris Taylor, MD
